prompt: USER EMPATHY RESEARCH, 2026. A company sells software to enterprises. They added AI to it (a copilot, an assistant, an agent, automation, AI features). Now their enterprise deals stall in the customer's SECURITY REVIEW because the customer's security team is worried about the AI: what it can access, whether it leaks data, whether it can be manipulated. WHO is the person inside the selling company who feels this pain and would go buy a tool to fix it? Give the realistic personas (role/title: founder, head of eng, CISO, security engineer, AE/sales eng, GTM lead?), what kind of company (AI-native startup vs SaaS-adding-AI vs enterprise platform), and CRUCIALLY how THEY describe what they built in their own words. Key question: do real people say 'our AI agent' or do they say 'our product', 'our platform', 'our app', 'our AI feature', 'the AI in our product'? Quote real 2026 posts/threads (@handle + date) and real language. Conclude: the single unit/noun the buyer most identifies with (product? platform? agent? feature?), and the persona most likely to self-serve buy.

USER EMPATHY: capture the FELT PAIN in the buyer's own words. A software company added AI to their product and now an enterprise deal is stuck because the customer's security/procurement team is scrutinizing the AI. Find real 2026 posts, threads, Reddit/HN comments, and Slack/forum venting (quote verbatim with @handle/source + date) where founders, engineers, or security people express frustration like: 'security review is killing our AI deal', 'the customer's security team wants to know if our AI is safe', 'we keep getting AI security questionnaires', 'enterprise wants proof our AI wont leak their data', 'we shipped AI and now every deal needs a security review', 'how do we prove our chatbot/agent/copilot is secure'. What words and phrases do they actually use? What is the emotional core (frustration? deal velocity? feeling unprepared? not knowing what buyers want)? List the 12 most resonant verbatim phrases.

POSITIONING UNIT TEST, 2026. kolm signs verifiable SECURITY evidence about the AI a company ships, so the company can clear the enterprise security review faster. The current site leads with 'your AI agent' (e.g. 'security evidence for your AI agent'). HYPOTHESIS to validate or refute: this is too narrow/abstract because builders do not identify as having 'an AI agent' - they identify with their PRODUCT / PLATFORM / APP / the AI FEATURE they shipped. Which framing makes the most buyers immediately self-identify and think 'that is my problem'? Compare these framings for relatability + breadth + clarity: (a) 'your AI agent', (b) 'the AI in your product', (c) 'your AI feature', (d) 'your product/platform' (with AI implied), (e) 'your AI app', (f) 'your AI product'. Cite how real 2026 AI companies + security/trust vendors refer to the thing being secured. Recommend the single most relatable, broadest-yet-specific unit to anchor the positioning on, and 3 reasons. Note where 'agent' is still right (autonomous, acts on its own) vs where it is too narrow.

JOBS-TO-BE-DONE + OFF-THE-SHELF. For kolm (signed, verifiable security evidence about the AI a company ships, to clear enterprise security review in days not weeks), what is the JOB the buyer is hiring it for, in their language? Is it 'help me pass the security review', 'help me answer the AI security questionnaire', 'help me prove our AI is safe to customers', 'help me close enterprise deals faster', 'help me look enterprise-ready'? What would make this SELL OFF THE SHELF - i.e. a builder lands on the homepage and within 5 seconds thinks 'yes that is exactly my problem, let me try the free scan'? Identify the single sharpest JOB statement, the trigger moment they search for help, and the exact search terms / phrases they would type. Cite real 2026 examples of products that nail self-serve 'pass the security review / answer the questionnaire' positioning (Vanta Questionnaire Automation, SafeBase, security trust centers, etc., with URLs).

Given the empathy reality (a software company shipped AI in their PRODUCT/PLATFORM, and enterprise deals now stall in security review over that AI; the buyer identifies with their product, not 'an agent'; they want to pass the review / answer the AI security questionnaire / prove the AI is safe), write 8 RELATABLE, off-the-shelf homepage headline candidates that a product builder instantly recognizes as their problem. Constraints: security-led, plain and human (not jargon, not cryptographic-first, not 'agent'-narrow unless truly apt), no em/en dashes, do not overclaim that the AI is 'safe/secure/certified'. Anchor on the product/AI-feature the buyer identifies with and the security-review/questionnaire pain. For each headline give the 1-line subhead and why a real buyer relates. Then rank them and name the single best plus 2 alternates.

How do the most RELATABLE 2026 security/trust products talk to a software company that added AI and now faces enterprise security scrutiny? Study the homepage + messaging of Vanta (Questionnaire Automation, AI/Trust), SafeBase / trust centers, Conveyor, Drata, and any AI-specific trust/security-evidence products, plus how AI companies describe their own security on /security and /trust pages. Quote their actual hero lines + how they name the buyer's pain (cite URLs). What is the most relatable, human pain-framing in this category (e.g. 'close deals faster', 'stop drowning in security questionnaires', 'build trust', 'show customers your security')? What relatable frame can kolm adopt for the AI-specific version that is broad enough to sell off the shelf but distinct from generic GRC? Recommend the framing + the emotional hook.
